title: Epitaph. Intended for Sir Isaac Newton, in Westminster Abbey. by Alexander Pope — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
ISAACUS NEWTONUS:
    QUEM IMMORTALEM
TESTANTUR TEMPUS, NATURA, COELUM:
      MORTALEM
    HOC MARMOR FATETUR.

Nature and Nature's laws lay hid in night
God said, Let Newton be! and all was light.
meaning: "Epitaph. Intended for Sir Isaac Newton, in Westminster Abbey." by Alexander Pope is a deep poem consisting of 8 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "    ISAACUS NEWTONUS:     QUEM IMMORTALEM...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Alexander Pope's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
